channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: UK Imposes Sanctions on Networks Recruiting Kenyans for Russian Military Operations
item.description: The United Kingdom has introduced new sanctions targeting networks allegedly involved in recruiting migrants, including Kenyans, to support Russia's military efforts in Ukraine. The sanctions hit 35 i...

item.title: Publishers File Lawsuit Alleging Meta Used Copyrighted Works to Train AI
item.description: Five publishing companies, along with author Scott Turow, have filed a class action lawsuit against Meta and its CEO, Mark Zuckerberg, claiming the tech giant illegally used millions of copyrighted bo...

item.title: Reviving South Africa’s smallholder irrigation schemes to secure food and climate resilience
item.description: For many rural areas in South Africa, especially the Eastern Cape, land is lying idle even though irrigation infrastructure exists. This is because canals and pumps are broken or poorly managed, and w...

item.title: Majority of Filipinos Express Trust and Satisfaction in PNP, OCTA Survey Shows
item.description: According to the latest Tugon ng Masa survey conducted by OCTA, the Philippine National Police (PNP) enjoys significant trust and satisfaction among adult Filipinos in the first quarter of 2026. The s...

item.title: Vijay’s TVK requests Congress support after emerging as largest party in Tamil Nadu elections
item.description: In the recent Tamil Nadu Assembly elections, the actor-turned-politician Vijay’s Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) made an impressive debut by becoming the single-largest party in the state. Winning 108 ...

item.title: Alan Kohler on Labor’s Tax Reforms and the Impact on Housing Affordability
item.description: Alan Kohler, financial commentator and author, has discussed the upcoming changes to negative gearing and the capital gains tax discount as announced in the 2026 federal budget. These reforms are aime...

item.title: Alto Estimates Proposed Montreal-Ottawa High-Speed Rail May Affect 1,700 Properties
item.description: The proposed high-speed rail network connecting Montreal and Ottawa is expected to impact approximately 1,700 properties, including around 500 agricultural lands, according to the Crown corporation le...

item.title: Presidency Requests 30-Day Extension on National Water Crisis Plan Documents
item.description: The Democratic Alliance has criticised the Presidency for requesting a 30-day extension on a PAIA application seeking details about South Africa's National Water Crisis Plan. The request was lodged on...

item.title: Malaria Influenced Human Evolution and Settlement Patterns in Africa
item.description: Recent research from the Max Planck Institute of Geoanthropology and the University of Cambridge suggests that malaria, one of the oldest human infectious diseases, significantly influenced the evolut...

item.title: Top Fashion Moments from the 2026 Met Gala
item.description: The 2026 Met Gala for fashion no be small tin. Celebrities show up with plenty bold, creative, and elegant styles wey catch everybody eye. Beyoncé return after 10 years with Jay-Z and their daughter B...

item.title: TikTok Campaign Seeks to Crowdfund Spirit Airlines Amid Closure
item.description: A TikTok campaign to crowdfund the revival of Spirit Airlines has gained significant attention, securing pledges totalling over $132 million, although this represents non-binding promises rather than ...

item.title: Understanding the Potential Cancer Risks of Vaping: Insights from an Oncologist
item.description: Vaping, or using e-cigarettes, was originally created in the early 2000s by a Chinese pharmacist as a nicotine delivery device intended to be a safer alternative to traditional tobacco cigarettes, esp...

item.title: Islamabad High Court removes travel restrictions on YouTuber Rajab Butt
item.description: The Islamabad High Court (IHC) has ruled in favour of popular YouTuber Rajab Butt, ordering that his name be removed from the Passport Control List (PCL). The court observed that continuing travel res...

item.title: Makabayan Group Criticizes Kalinga Bill as Temporary Fix for High Inflation Rate
item.description: According to the latest report from ABS-CBN News, the Makabayan bloc in the House of Representatives has described the proposed Kalinga bill as nothing more than a 'band-aid response' to the country's...

item.title: The Manila Times Announces Passing of Chairman Emeritus Dante Arevalo Ang
item.description: The Manila Times has announced the passing of its Chairman Emeritus and journalist, Dante Arevalo Ang. The newspaper expressed deep sorrow, highlighting Ang's contributions as a father, journalist, pu...

item.title: Treasurer remains non-committal on potential tax offset for Australian workers
item.description: Ahead of next week’s Federal Budget, reports suggest the Australian Government may introduce an 'earned income offset' of between $200 and $300 for wage and salary earners to help manage the cost-of-l...

item.title: Non-payment of FASTag at barrier-less toll plazas may suspend vehicle services until dues are cleared
item.description: The government has clarified that failure to pay tolls through FASTag at barrier-less toll plazas on the National Highway network will lead to suspension of all FASTag-linked services for the concerne...

item.title: KCB Bank boosts Tanzania–Kenya trade ties amid regional integration efforts
item.description: KCB Bank has pledged to play a stronger role in connecting Tanzania and Kenya through trade and investment, acting as a key financial bridge for regional economic integration. The bank’s CEO, Paul Rus...

item.title: Concerns Over Long-Term Zoning Monopoly in Gombe State Politics
item.description: Since Nigeria return to democracy for 1999, Gombe State don dey show say political power dey concentrated for Gombe North, particularly Gombe Local Government. For 24 years now, governors from this on...

item.title: University cleaner awarded £264,000 after unfair dismissal dispute
item.description: A 72-year-old part-time cleaner, Peak Ong, has been awarded over £260,000 in compensation after an employment tribunal ruled she was unfairly dismissed from Aberystwyth University. Ong, who worked at ...
